verdict: DIVERGENCE CONFIRMED: compressibility is empirically distinct from D1 finality (trace_survival) and information-theoretic loss (lost_bits) in this model. The direction finality→records→predictability→compression is validated. Compression-survival correlation: -0.745. Witnesses: Rules 15 and 166 have similar trace_survival (1.00) but compressibility gap 0.50 (0.54 vs 0.04). Rules 2 and 8 have similar lost_bits (2.59) but compressibility gap 0.60 (0.40 vs 1.00).
recommendation: If divergence is confirmed: add compressibility as an observable alongside D1 in the emergence lab and report its correlation with each D1 dimension separately. Do NOT promote compressibility to a D1 dimension — report it as a derived observable that partially tracks finality. If reversible rules show higher compressibility than irreversible rules with similar survival, that is the strongest evidence that compressibility and D1 finality are genuinely different axes.
